hypothesis: A small linear model on lagged returns, volatility, moving-average gap, and funding can rank hourly Hyperliquid names better than naive long-short selection.
Baseline agent run: ridge model on hourly lagged returns, volatility, moving-average gap, and funding using the fixed Hyperliquid judge.
Lower L2 regularization from 5.0 to 1.0 to allow stronger model coefficients. Baseline coefficients are tiny (~1e-4), suggesting over-regularization is killing the signal.
Double the training window from 504 to 1008 bars (42 days). More training data should produce a more stable and better-calibrated model.
Use rank transform for all features instead of zscore. Rank is more robust to outliers and produces uniform cross-sectional distributions, which may improve signal quality for a linear model.
Building on rank-all success (outer active_sharpe=0.88). Increase position_bucket from 4 to 5 for more diversification and potentially lower concentration risk.
Further increase position_bucket from 5 to 6. Bucket=5 improved both Sharpe (0.95) and turnover (0.25). Test if bucket=6 continues the trend.
Simplify to the 3 strongest signals from bucket=5 run: ret_1h (reversal), ma_gap_24h (mean-reversion), funding_8h (carry). Drop weaker momentum and volatility features to reduce overfitting.
Funding carry is the dominant signal (coef=-0.0056). Test longer funding lookback (24h vs 8h) for a smoother, more stable carry signal.
Add ret_6h (6h momentum, rank) back to the best 3-feature model. In the 6-feature run it had positive coefficient (+0.00075). Testing whether this momentum signal adds value alongside reversal/carry.
Lower L2 from 5.0 to 1.0 on the best 3-feature model. With only 3 features the overfitting risk is low, so less regularization may let the model express stronger views.
Switch target from next_open_to_close to next_close_to_close return. Close-to-close captures the full bar return including the gap, which may be more aligned with actual P&L.
Try OLS (no regularization) instead of ridge. With only 3 features and 1526 training rows (500:1 ratio), overfitting risk is minimal. OLS may produce a more efficient estimator.
Test ma_gap with 48h lookback instead of 24h. A longer MA captures more persistent mean-reversion and may be better aligned with 24h rebalance cadence.
Shorten training window from 504 to 336 bars (14 days). Shorter window adapts faster to regime changes, which may improve inner-period performance where the model currently struggles.
Test 2-feature model with just funding_8h (strongest signal, coef=-0.013) and ret_1h (reversal, coef=-0.001). Drop ma_gap_24h to test if it adds real value or noise.
Increase rebalance from 24h to 48h. May improve stability and reduce turnover further while the signal (dominated by funding carry) persists across days.
Add 168h (1-week) return as 4th feature. This captures weekly momentum/reversal at a different time scale from the 1h reversal signal. May add uncorrelated alpha.
